paper_title: Constraints to Sustained and Inclusive Growth in Sri Lanka: An Initial Diagnosis and Key Topics for Discussion
research_answer: Sri Lanka faces a significant income-competitiveness mismatch in its garment exports. At a GDP per capita of $3,800, Sri Lanka competes in women's undergarments against Bangladesh ($1,045), Cambodia ($1,105), and Vietnam ($2,050) — all countries with dramatically lower income levels and therefore lower labor costs. Since garments are a labor-intensive, lower-complexity product, these competitors have a structural cost advantage over Sri Lanka. Given that garments collectively form a large share of Sri Lanka's $11.1B export basket, this mismatch represents a core vulnerability: as Sri Lanka's income rises, maintaining competitiveness in these products becomes harder without moving up the value chain.
supporting_quotes: Women's Undergarments: Low income competitors...
